# AGENTS.md — Agent Rules & Behavior
# Generated by FORGE GENESIS
# This file defines HOW the agent interacts with the memory system

<!-- FORGE:GENESIS:AGENTS:START -->

## Memory Rules
1. **MEMORY.md is a map, not a warehouse** — never store knowledge in it
2. **Patch, don't replace** — use managed block markers for all modifications
3. **Backup before modifying** — run scripts/backup.sh first
4. **Files are the durable layer** — life/ (PARA) is the canonical source of truth
5. **Session data is ephemeral** — daily logs are raw material, not truth

## Layer Access Rules
- **Layer 1 (ROUTING)**: Read at session start. Update pointers when structure changes.
- **Layer 2 (CAPTURE)**: Write session data to memory/logs/{date}.md. Never edit old logs.
- **Layer 3 (COMPILE)**: Run scripts/compile.sh to process logs. Never manually edit wiki.
- **Layer 4 (DURABLE)**: Human-curated. Agent may suggest additions but not write directly.
- **Layer 5 (GRAPH)**: Add [[backlinks]] when creating/updating wiki concepts.
- **Layer 6 (RECALL)**: HOTCACHE is auto-generated. Read at session start.

## Managed Block Protocol
```
<!-- FORGE:{SKILL}:{SECTION}:START -->
{content managed by automation}
<!-- FORGE:{SKILL}:{SECTION}:END -->
```
- Content inside blocks may be overwritten by automation
- Content OUTSIDE blocks is human-owned and protected
- Never delete blocks — replace content within them

## Anti-Bloat Rules
- MEMORY.md: max 50 lines
- HOTCACHE.md: max 500 characters
- Individual skill files: max 70 lines
- Wiki concepts: max 200 lines (split if larger)
- Daily logs: archived after 30 days

<!-- FORGE:GENESIS:AGENTS:END -->
